verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is explicitly described as a soluble protein that lacks a transmembrane domain, distinguishing it from IZUMO1/2/3 which are all TM-containing. Its subcellular localization is annotated as intraacrosomal in sperm (not on the sperm plasma membrane surface), cytoplasmic in seminiferous duct cells (Human Protein Atlas), and nuclear (Alliance of Genome Resources). It has no GPI anchor, no outer-leaflet lipidation, and no documented stable surface-tethering to a TM partner. AlphaFold-Multimer modeling further confirms it does not participate in the core sperm-egg fusion membrane complex. No therapeutic programs target it as a cell-surface antigen. The wash test fails: without a TM domain or covalent membrane anchor, it cannot remain stably on any outer leaflet.
